question: A developer starts the Docker Compose lightweight configuration and opens `http://localhost:8070` to use Web Modeler, but the page does not load. What is required to use Web Modeler with the lightweight setup?

options.text: Restart the lightweight stack with `docker compose up -d --profile web-modeler` to enable the bundled Web Modeler service.
Run `docker compose -f docker-compose-web-modeler.yaml up -d` separately, then manually configure the cluster connection in Web Modeler using `NONE` or `BASIC` authentication.
Set `WEB_MODELER_ENABLED=true` in the `.env` file and restart the lightweight stack to activate Web Modeler.
Switch to the full configuration; Web Modeler cannot run alongside the lightweight Orchestration Cluster on the same machine.

a.text: There is no `--profile web-modeler` toggle on the lightweight compose file. Web Modeler is not bundled with the lightweight configuration and cannot be turned on with a Compose profile.

b.text: Web Modeler is not included in the lightweight configuration. The docs instruct you to run `docker-compose-web-modeler.yaml` separately and configure the cluster connection by hand, using `NONE` or `BASIC` authentication because the lightweight Orchestration Cluster does not run Keycloak/OIDC.

c.text: There is no `WEB_MODELER_ENABLED` environment variable on the lightweight stack. Web Modeler ships as a separate compose file, not as a flag enabled on the lightweight setup.

d.text: Web Modeler can run alongside the lightweight Orchestration Cluster — it just has to come from `docker-compose-web-modeler.yaml` and be wired to the cluster manually. Switching to the full configuration is one option, but not a requirement.

explanation: Web Modeler is only included in the full Docker Compose configuration by default. To use it with the lightweight setup, start Web Modeler independently via `docker compose -f docker-compose-web-modeler.yaml up -d` and configure the cluster connection by hand. Because the lightweight Orchestration Cluster does not run Keycloak/OIDC, the connection must use `NONE` or `BASIC` authentication rather than OAuth.
